title: Be nice and be specific. Our volunteers are Mozilla users just like you who give their own time to help. Telling us “YouTube videos are always choppy” is much more helpful than “something is broken”.
content:
1. Резюме выделенного
      1. Общее

Выделенный фрагмент содержит **цепочку из трёх X.509 TLS-сертификатов** (формат PEM), а также временну́ю метку **«5–7 минут»** (вероятно, срок действия или контекст страницы).

---

      1. Сертификат 1 — Конечный (End-Entity)

- **Субъект:** `accounts.firefox.com` - **Выдан:** Let's Encrypt (R13) - **Действителен:** 15.02.2026 — 16.05.2026 - **Тип:** TLS-сертификат сервера (Domain Validated) - **Алгоритм:** RSA 2048 / SHA-256

---

      1. Сертификат 2 — Промежуточный (Intermediate CA)

- **Субъект:** `R13` (Let's Encrypt) - **Выдан:** ISRG Root X1 - **Действителен:** 13.03.2024 — 12.03.2027 - **Назначение:** Подпись конечных сертификатов Let's Encrypt

---

      1. Сертификат 3 — Корневой (Root CA)

- **Субъект:** `ISRG Root X1` (Internet Security Research Group) - **Действителен:** 04.06.2015 — 04.06.2035 - **Назначение:** Доверенный корневой центр сертификации; самоподписанный

---

      1. Итог

Полная цепочка доверия: `accounts.firefox.com` → `Let's Encrypt R13` → `ISRG Root X1`

title: How do I stop search jumping to URL box in a new tab
content: Until the most recent update, when I opened a new tab a box with the message "Search the web" appeared, and I could enter a query to search. This was exactly what I wanted. Now, the box still appears, but as soon as I type a character the input jumps to the small box at the top left where I would expect to enter a URL. I don't like this, because (a) the jump is disconcerting when I'm trying to type, (b) I want what I type in the URL box to be always taken as a URL, not as a search query, (c) the box in the middle of the new tab is just a waste of space: something held out then snatched away. The solution I have found suggested is to set the resource browser.newtabpage.activity-stream.improvesearch.handoffToAwesomebar to false, but this causes an error message to appear in the search box. I'm sure there must be a way to have the old behaviour back - any help gratefully received.

I am experiencing an issue with Firefox where some websites display a message saying that the internet connection cannot be established and that I am offline, even though I am connected to the internet.

Thank you.
